title: How I Found and Fixed 5 Critical Lighthouse Issues in a WebGL 3D Landing Page (With a Python CLI)
content:
A few months ago, I was building a B2B landing page that embedded a 3D industrial model using <model-viewer>. The page looked great visually. Then I ran Lighthouse.

**Score: 42/100.**

Here are the 5 issues I found -- and what made each one non-obvious:

## 1. Too Many Font Weights (8 requests instead of 3)

Google Fonts was loading wght@300;400;500;600;700;800 -- 6 weights for Inter plus 2 for JetBrains Mono = **8 separate HTTP requests just for fonts**.

Each one is render-blocking. The fix: reduce to wght@400;600;700. Saved ~400ms.

## 2. <model-viewer> Script in <head> (Render-Blocking)

The WebGL script was 300KB. Sitting in <head>. Blocking everything.

Fix: Move it to the end of <body>. Use type="module" so it defers automatically.

```html
<!-- Before (in <head>) -->
<script src="model-viewer.min.js" type="module"></script>

<!-- After (end of <body>) -->
<script type="module" src="model-viewer.min.js"></script>
```

## 3. Missing preconnect and dns-prefetch

The page used Google Fonts, a QR code API, and the model-viewer CDN -- but had zero preconnect tags. Each domain needed a full DNS lookup + TLS handshake before any resource could load.

Fix: Add preconnect for all external domains in <head>.

## 4. Hero Image Set to loading="lazy" 

Someone (me) had applied loading="lazy" to ALL images including the hero image at the top of the page. The browser would defer loading the most important image -- the one that determines LCP.

Fix: Hero image gets loading="eager" and fetchpriority="high". Everything below the fold stays lazy.

## 5. The Silent SEO Killer: JavaScript Injected Into JSON-LD

This one is the nastiest bug.

A Particle.js animation was running on the page. Due to a timing issue, it appended animation callback code into a <script type="application/ld+json"> block. The page looked perfect in the browser. No console errors.

But the JSON-LD was now invalid. Google Search Console quietly stopped indexing the Product rich snippet. I only found out 3 months later when I ran a structured data audit.

---

## The Fix: web-performance-surgeon

I got tired of hunting these issues manually, so I built a Python CLI that scans and auto-fixes all of them:

```bash
# Audit only -- shows all issues
python web_performance_surgeon.py index.html

# Audit + auto-fix everything
python web_performance_surgeon.py index.html --fix
```

Final score after fixes: **98/100 Lighthouse**.

I also built companion tools for the other issues:
- **json-ld-schema-guardian** -- specifically scans for JS injection into JSON-LD blocks
- **static-dom-surgeon** -- surgically patches HTML elements at scale (used BeautifulSoup to patch 21 product cards without touching surrounding code)
- **b2b-landing-page-checklist** -- 50-point pre-launch checklist with all the lessons from this project

All free and open source: [github.com/tanphan1105](https://github.com/tanphan1105)

---

*Have you run into the JSON-LD injection bug before? Curious if this is more common than I think.*
